description_long: This object is a brown pine cone, featuring tightly packed, overlapping woody scales that spiral around a central axis. The scales are robust and have a slightly curved, pointed shape, with some lighter patches of color at the tips, indicating natural weathering. The pine cone is elongated, with one end tapering to a point and the other end slightly broader and more rounded. Its surface is rough and textured, with visible ridges and grooves along each scale. The overall coloration ranges from deep brown to lighter tan, with subtle variations across the surface. This type of tree debris is commonly found on the ground in urban parks, sidewalks, or near coniferous trees.
description: A brown, elongated pine cone with tightly packed, overlapping woody scales and a rough, textured surface. The scales are pointed and spiral around a central axis, showing natural color variations.
description_view_0: A rounded, broader end of the pine cone with scales fanning outward and overlapping densely.
description_view_1: A side profile showing the full length of the pine cone, highlighting the pointed, tapered end and the spiral arrangement of the scales.
description_view_2: An angled view emphasizing the lighter tips of the scales and the elongated body of the pine cone.
description_view_3: A side view displaying the dense, overlapping scales and the overall cylindrical shape of the pine cone.
